title: Mehta Fabricators vs. IndoSteel Components
facts: MSME supply dispute involving defective industrial components. Latent defect confirmed by third-party inspection. Defect liability clause (Clause 7.2) disputed. Business production stoppage for 3 months claimed. Supplier failed to respond within 30-day window.
disputed_clause: Latent defect liability in supply contracts
outcome: Settled at Rs 1.95L. Defect liability clause enforced. Third-party inspection report accepted as evidence.
key_principle: Latent defects in industrial supply impose full replacement liability on supplier when confirmed by independent inspection.

title: Rajan Auto Parts vs. Chennai Precision Works
facts: Supply agreement dispute for auto components. Production loss claimed due to defective parts. Supplier did not respond to legal notice for 5 months. Adverse inference drawn from supplier silence.
disputed_clause: Defect replacement and production loss liability
outcome: Awarded Rs 2.1L. Silent supplier adverse inference applied. 84% of claimed amount recovered.
key_principle: Supplier silence for 4+ months creates strong adverse inference. Claimant position significantly strengthened.

title: Gupta Textiles vs. Rajasthan Dye Works
facts: Dispute over defective dye process causing fabric damage. Partial defect admitted by supplier. No formal legal notice sent before initiating dispute. Production records incomplete.
disputed_clause: Process quality guarantee and defect liability
outcome: Settled at Rs 1.42L. Absence of formal notice weakened bargaining severely. Only 44% recovery.
key_principle: Formal legal notice is mandatory before claiming settlement. Without it, bargaining position weakens significantly.

title: Kapoor Electronics vs. Delhi Component Suppliers
facts: Consumer dispute over electronic components failing within warranty period. Batch defect confirmed. Manufacturer failed to honor warranty replacement. Consumer suffered financial losses.
disputed_clause: Warranty and replacement obligation
outcome: Rs 1.45L awarded. Warranty obligation strictly enforced. Rapid resolution due to clear evidence.
key_principle: Clear warranty documentation and batch defect confirmation leads to high win probability in consumer disputes.

title: Sharma Construction vs. BuildMat Pvt Ltd
facts: Construction materials supplied were substandard. Contract specified IS grade steel but inferior quality delivered. Site inspection confirmed material non-compliance. Project delayed 4 months.
disputed_clause: Material specification compliance clause
outcome: Settled at Rs 2.9L including project delay costs. IS standard non-compliance treated as fundamental breach.
key_principle: Delivery of non-specification materials constitutes fundamental breach. Delay costs recoverable when causation is proven.

title: Patel Pharma vs. ChemSource India
facts: Pharmaceutical raw material supplied failed quality control testing. Batch rejected by drug inspector. Supplier denied liability claiming storage conditions at buyer premises. Third-party lab test confirmed supplier fault.
disputed_clause: Quality certification and batch liability
outcome: Rs 3.8L awarded. Supplier storage defence rejected when independent lab confirmed latent defect.
key_principle: Independent laboratory confirmation overrides supplier storage defence. Quality certification binds supplier fully.

title: Verma Packaging vs. PrintPro Solutions
facts: Printing services contracted for packaging. Final product color mismatch and structural defects. Client suffered inventory rejection from retail partner. Supplier offered partial redo but client refused citing commercial loss.
disputed_clause: Service quality guarantee and consequential loss
outcome: Settled at Rs 72K. Consequential loss claim partially rejected. Direct defect loss compensated.
key_principle: Consequential business losses from service defects are only partially recoverable. Direct defect costs are standard.

title: Agarwal Motors vs. AutoParts Wholesale
facts: Auto parts supplied had manufacturing defects causing vehicle warranty claims. OEM certification misrepresented. Criminal complaint filed alongside civil claim. Settlement negotiated to avoid criminal proceedings.
disputed_clause: OEM certification misrepresentation
outcome: Rs 4.2L settled. Criminal complaint threat accelerated settlement. OEM misrepresentation treated as fraud.
key_principle: Misrepresentation of certifications elevates dispute to potential fraud. Criminal complaint significantly improves bargaining position.

title: Nair Plastics vs. Chennai Chemicals
facts: Supplier defaulted on payment for goods already delivered and accepted. Buyer raised quality dispute retrospectively after payment demand. Forum found quality dispute was an afterthought to avoid payment.
disputed_clause: Payment terms and quality dispute timeline
outcome: Rs 2.65L recovered. Retrospective quality disputes after payment demand rejected. Supplier paid almost full amount.
key_principle: Quality disputes raised only after payment is demanded are viewed as bad faith. Courts favor suppliers with accepted delivery proof.

title: Singh Hospitality vs. FoodEquip Pvt Ltd
facts: Commercial kitchen equipment failed within 6 months of installation. Manufacturer blamed misuse. CCTV footage showed normal usage. Extended downtime caused business revenue loss claimed.
disputed_clause: Commercial warranty and business loss
outcome: Settled at Rs 1.95L. Business revenue loss only partially awarded. Equipment replacement cost fully compensated.
key_principle: Business revenue loss claims require strong documentation. Equipment replacement costs are core and easier to recover.

title: Reddy Construction vs. Steel Mart India
facts: Steel bars delivered below contracted tensile strength. Civil engineer certified non-compliance. Demolition and rework costs claimed. Supplier claimed transportation damage caused the defect.
disputed_clause: Delivery quality and transportation risk transfer
outcome: Settled at Rs 5.1L. Transportation risk held with supplier until site acceptance. Engineer report decisive.
key_principle: Risk of quality defects transfers to buyer only after formal site acceptance. Engineer certification is key evidence.

title: Tiwari Electronics vs. PowerMax Batteries
facts: Battery units supplied exploded causing property damage. Product liability claim filed. Supplier claimed improper installation. Installation was done by supplier-certified technicians.
disputed_clause: Product liability and installation responsibility
outcome: Rs 3.8L awarded. Supplier-certified installer eliminates installation defense. Near full recovery.
key_principle: When supplier provides certified installers, product liability rests fully with supplier. Installation defence eliminated.

title: Kumar Textiles vs. Dye House Express
facts: Fabric processing caused color bleeding failing export quality norms. Export order cancelled by foreign buyer. Consequential export loss claimed in addition to processing charges.
disputed_clause: Export quality compliance and consequential loss
outcome: Settled at Rs 2.8L. Export consequential losses significantly discounted. Processing fee loss directly compensated.
key_principle: Export consequential losses face high evidentiary bar. Direct processing losses are readily compensable.

title: Bose Pharma Distributors vs. MediSupply Corp
facts: Short expiry pharmaceutical products supplied. Distributor unable to sell products. Storage and disposal costs incurred. Supplier denied responsibility claiming expiry was within contract terms.
disputed_clause: Minimum shelf life requirement in supply contract
outcome: Rs 1.75L settled. Minimum shelf life implied term recognised even without explicit contract clause.
key_principle: Minimum shelf life for pharmaceutical products is an implied term. Supplier responsibility exists even without explicit clause.

title: Jain Garments vs. Fabric House Mumbai
facts: Fabric supplied not of contracted GSM weight. Lab test confirmed 20% GSM shortfall. Supplier claimed test methodology was flawed. Accredited lab test accepted by forum.
disputed_clause: GSM specification and testing standards
outcome: Rs 1.28L recovered. Accredited lab test conclusive. Supplier challenge to methodology rejected.
key_principle: Accredited laboratory test results are conclusive evidence. Supplier methodology challenges without counter-expert evidence are rejected.

title: Desai Engineering vs. Hydraulics Plus
facts: Hydraulic systems supplied failed to meet contracted pressure specifications. Field test failure documented. Supplier offered repair but client rejected citing safety concerns. Safety documentation supported client position.
disputed_clause: Performance specification compliance and repair option
outcome: Rs 6.2L settled. Safety-critical specification failure allows rejection over repair. High amount reflects safety premium.
key_principle: Failure to meet safety-critical specifications entitles buyer to reject repair option and claim full replacement. Safety documentation is crucial.

title: Pandey Cold Storage vs. Refricon Systems
facts: Cold storage installation failed maintaining temperature. Perishable goods spoiled. AMC contract in place but service response delayed by 72 hours. Temperature logs documented failure.
disputed_clause: AMC response time and spoilage liability
outcome: Rs 2.2L settled. 72-hour response breach of AMC terms. Spoilage directly linked to response delay.
key_principle: AMC response time breach directly causing perishable loss creates clear liability. Temperature logs are decisive evidence.

title: Mittal Steel Processing vs. Industrial Coatings Ltd
facts: Anti-corrosion coating failed within 6 months against 5-year guarantee. Outdoor installation as specified. Supplier claimed unusual weather conditions. Meteorological data showed normal conditions.
disputed_clause: Coating performance guarantee and force majeure
outcome: Rs 2.1L settled. Force majeure weather defence rejected by meteorological data. Guarantee enforced.
key_principle: Supplier force majeure defence requires extraordinary events. Normal weather conditions cannot void performance guarantees.

title: Suresh Packaging vs. Poly Films India
facts: Packaging films supplied had micro-perforations causing product spoilage for client's customers. Multi-tier liability claim. Intermediate supplier traced defect to raw material supplier.
disputed_clause: Quality assurance in supply chain
outcome: Rs 2.4L settled. Intermediate supplier held partially liable. Multi-party dispute reduced individual recovery.
key_principle: Multi-party supply chain disputes reduce individual recovery percentages. Direct supplier relationship is strongest basis for claim.

title: Trivedi Hotels vs. Laundry Pro Services
facts: Commercial laundry service damaged luxury linen worth significant amount. Hotel claimed replacement cost plus brand damage. Service provider claimed normal wear. Expert textile assessment confirmed abnormal damage.
disputed_clause: Liability cap for service-related damage
outcome: Rs 1.42L awarded. Liability cap clauses in service contracts partially enforced. Expert assessment decisive.
key_principle: Liability caps in service contracts are partially enforceable. Expert assessment of damage quantum is influential in final award.
